page: Vultr voc-s-8c-64gb-1920s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring an AMD EPYC Milan processor with 8 vCPUs and 8 physical cores running at 2.0 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance allocates shared CPU resources and provides 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage is bundled with 1920 GB of NVMe SSD capacity, while no dedicated GPU hardware is present.

Performance benchmarks reveal a strong single-core capability, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance ranking in the top tier. However, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth tests, including both cached and uncached read/write operations, fall within the average tier. Database operations, Redis SET performance at 15.09 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput at 8.4 GB/sec also align with average tier expectations. For machine learning workloads, the server handles small 135M model inference at average speeds, but struggles with medium 7B model prompt processing, which registers in the bottom tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this server offers a high density of memory and NVMe storage relative to its shared CPU allocation. The shared CPU model represents a tradeoff, making it less suitable for sustained, intensive multi-core computations but highly economical for memory-heavy and storage-reliant workloads. Recommended applications include database hosting, caching layers, web serving, and general-purpose workloads requiring significant memory and fast local storage without continuous maximum CPU utilization.
description: Vultr voc-s-8c-64gb-1920s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server powered by an AMD EPYC Milan CPU with 8 vCPUs and 8 physical cores at 2.0 GHz. It features 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a generous 8.0 GB per core, alongside a bundled 1920 GB NVMe SSD for fast local storage. While single-core CPU performance ranks in the top tier, multi-core execution, memory bandwidth, and database operations perform at average levels. The shared CPU allocation presents a tradeoff for sustained multi-threaded workloads, but the high memory and storage density make it a cost-effective option. This configuration is well-suited for database hosting, caching layers, static web serving, and memory-intensive applications.
og_description: Vultr voc-s-8c-64gb-1920s-amd is an AMD EPYC Milan server with 8 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 1920 GB NVMe storage. It delivers top-tier single-core performance and average multi-core speeds for database and web workloads.
meta_description: Vultr voc-s-8c-64gb-1920s-amd features 8 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 1920 GB NVMe SSD storage, offering balanced performance for database workloads.
tagline: An AMD-powered virtual server combining high-capacity NVMe storage and dense memory allocation for database and caching workloads.

64.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
